text: A 77-year-old man with metastatic prostate cancer reports worsening back pain. He is irritable and demands medications exactly on time. Most likely explanation?[span_0](end_span)
rationale: Dx: Undertreated pain. [span_1](start_span)Risk: Irritability in cancer patients often suggests inadequate pain control rather than addiction[span_1](end_span).

text: A 21-year-old man presents with status epilepticus (seizure >5 mins). [span_2](start_span)What is the first-line pharmacotherapy?[span_2](end_span)
rationale: Dx: GCSE. [span_3](start_span)Tx: Benzodiazepines (e.g., lorazepam) are first-line to terminate seizure activity[span_3](end_span).

text: A 4-year-old boy has fever, sore throat, neck stiffness, and trismus (unable to open mouth). [span_4](start_span)Likely diagnosis?[span_4](end_span)
rationale: Dx: Retropharyngeal abscess (RPA). [span_5](start_span)Risk: Common in children aged 2-4 after a respiratory infection[span_5](end_span).

text: A 56-year-old IV drug user has palpable purpura, arthralgia, and positive cryoglobulins. [span_6](start_span)Next best test?[span_6](end_span)
rationale: Dx: Cryoglobulinemic vasculitis. [span_7](start_span)Tx: 90% are associated with Hepatitis C infection[span_7](end_span).

text: A woman taking lithium for bipolar disorder is pregnant. [span_8](start_span)What fetal anomaly is most likely associated?[span_8](end_span)
rationale: Risk: Lithium in pregnancy increases the risk of Ebstein anomaly (cardiac)[span_9](end_span).

text: A 25-year-old female fears scrutiny at office parties and meeting new people. [span_10](start_span)Diagnosis?[span_10](end_span)
rationale: Dx: Social anxiety disorder (Social phobia)[span_11](end_span). [span_12](start_span)Tx: CBT is first-line[span_12](end_span).

text: 72yo with carotid bruit; Duplex shows complete occlusion of right ICA. [span_13](start_span)Best management?[span_13](end_span)
rationale: Tx: Medical therapy only. [span_14](start_span)Carotid endarterectomy (CEA) is not beneficial in complete occlusion[span_14](end_span).

text: Why do diagnostic tests have a higher PPV than screening tests?[span_15](end_span)
rationale: Dx: Higher prevalence enhances positive predictive value (PPV)[span_16](end_span).

text: A 2-year-old boy has a large amount of painless 'brick-red' rectal bleeding. [span_17](start_span)Diagnosis?[span_17](end_span)
rationale: Dx: Meckel diverticulum classically presents as painless brick-red bleeding[span_18](end_span).

text: 67yo man with knee 'pop', inferiorly displaced patella (patella baja), unable to extend knee. [span_19](start_span)Diagnosis?[span_19](end_span)
rationale: Dx: Quadriceps tendon rupture[span_20](end_span). [span_21](start_span)Note: Patellar tendon rupture causes patella alta (high-riding)[span_21](end_span).
